specific_mechanism: Returning forager honey bees perform a figure-8 dance on the hive comb. The straight-run direction relative to vertical encodes angle-to-source relative to the sun. Straight-run duration encodes distance. The dance is performed for many observers simultaneously; each receiver decodes the pose-and-tempo signature mechanically to extract two scalars (angle, distance). Receivers then fly to the indicated location.
llm_application: Inter-agent communication via compact pose-and-tempo signatures encoding a small fixed set of operationally important parameters (location-in-task-space, magnitude, urgency) rather than free-form natural language or rigid JSON. Senders emit structured signatures; receivers decode mechanically into action vectors. Designed for one-to-many decoding cheapness.
novelty_claim: Multi-agent LLM communication is heavily studied: TalkHier (2502.11098) introduces structured-communication-protocol; 'Multi-Agent Mark' (2505.21298) recommends KQML-style structured formats; Beyond Self-Talk survey (2502.14321) catalogs communication mechanisms; Multi-Agent Collaboration Survey (2501.06322). Bee-waggle-dance specifically is in robotics literature (RoboBee biomimetic dance, PMC12467162 Bio-Agentic Visual Communication for UAV swarms). The bee-waggle-as-LLM-multi-agent-communication framing is occupied by the UAV-swarm bio-inspired work.
